choices.text: interrupt() in node. Graph pauses; surfaces decision request to UI; user approves/rejects/edits; resume with decision. State persists via checkpointer during pause. Use for: risky actions (emails, payments, DB writes, customer comms). Selective: interrupt only when confidence low or risk high — balance automation + safety.

questions.explanation: interrupt() pauses graph; user decides via UI; resume.

questions.prompt: Agent sends customer emails autonomously; one mistake hurts brand. Add safety?

choices.text: Human approval interrupt before send. Pattern: agent drafts email → interrupt with draft → human reviews via UI → approve/edit/reject → if approved, send. Selective: low-stakes confirmations (order confirmed) auto-send; high-stakes (refund denial, complaint response) require approval. As confidence builds: gradually expand auto-send. Initial deployment: 100% approved; over time, approve patterns automatically as trust grows. Customer-facing AI without human-in-loop = recipe for PR disasters.

questions.explanation: Interrupt for approval before send. Selective by risk; expand auto-send as trust grows.
